name: CN Tower to Distillery
summary: A west-to-east overview of many of Toronto's most well-known attractions and historical sights.
next: To head back to the city centre, catch a 504 King streetcar on the far side of Cherry Street just east of the Distillery. Or consider the nearby Corktown Walk or Canary District Walk.

name: Old Town Toronto
summary: A clockwise circuit of Toronto's oldest neighbourhood.
next: From here, the King subway station is a short walk to the west. The 504 King streetcar runs west to the city centre, and the 504A King streetcar runs east to the Distillery District.

name: Canary District
summary: One of Toronto's newest neighbourhoods, built on industrial lands repurposed for the 2015 Pan Am Games.
next: To head back to the city centre, catch a 504 King streetcar in front of the Former Canary Restaurant. To visit the Distillery District, walk 5 minutes south on Cherry Street. Or consider the nearby Corktown Walk.

name: Corktown
summary: Explore one of Toronto's oldest neighbourhoods, a working class area built by Irish immigrants.
next: To head back to the city centre, catch a 504 King streetcar westbound on King Street. To visit the Distillery District, walk 5 minutes south on Cherry Street. Or consider the nearby Canary District Walk.
